title: The Fifth Sunday of Easter
context:
# TEMPORAL CONTEXT SNAPSHOT
Sermon: "The Fifth Sunday of Easter" | Saint John's Episcopal Cathedral, Denver
May 15, 2022 | Preacher: Broderick Greer

## 1. LITURGICAL CONTEXT

The Fifth Sunday of Easter falls within the Easter Season, specifically between Easter (April 10, 2022) and Ascension Day (May 26, 2022). Under the Revised Common Lectionary, the readings for this Sunday typically include Acts 11:1-18 (Peter's vision and the conversion of gentiles), Psalm 148, Revelation 21:1-6 (the new creation), and John 13:31-35 (Jesus's commandment to love one another). This period emphasizes resurrection joy, the inclusion of all peoples in God's kingdom, and Christ's final commandments to his disciples before the Ascension.

## 2. EPISCOPAL / ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS

The Episcopal Church was navigating ongoing tensions around LGBTQ+ inclusion and theological interpretation following the 2021 General Convention. The Diocese of Colorado, under Bishop Kimberly Chastain (elected 2020), was working to implement resolutions from the 81st General Convention. No major diocesan transitions or elections were occurring in May 2022, though the church continued post-pandemic reopening and attendance recovery discussions nationally.

## 3. DENVER & COLORADO NEWS

Denver was experiencing spring weather typical for mid-May, with temperatures in the 70s and occasional afternoon thunderstorms. The city was focused on the upcoming primary election season (Colorado's primary election was June 28, 2022), with local political campaigns intensifying. Downtown Denver and the Capitol Hill area near Saint John's Cathedral were in the midst of ongoing pandemic recovery in retail and hospitality sectors, with businesses fully reopened and downtown foot traffic increasing.

## 4. NATIONAL NEWS (US)

The Supreme Court's draft opinion overturning Roe v. Wade leaked on May 2, 2022, dominating national headlines and abortion rights protests throughout the first half of May. Monkeypox cases were beginning to be reported in the US, with the first confirmed case detected in Massachusetts on May 18 (just after this sermon). The Biden administration was managing inflation concerns and gas price spikes (averaging over $4 per gallon nationally), which was a leading domestic political issue.

## 5. INTERNATIONAL NEWS

Russia's invasion of Ukraine continued with heavy fighting in eastern Ukraine; Mariupol fell to Russian forces in mid-May after weeks of siege. The European Union was debating a proposed oil embargo against Russia as part of its sixth sanctions package. Severe drought conditions were affecting the Horn of Africa, particularly Somalia and Kenya, with aid organizations warning of potential famine conditions.

## 6. CULTURAL MOMENT

The national mood in mid-May 2022 was tense and polarized, centered on reproductive rights debates following the leaked Supreme Court draft and imminent Roe decision (announced June 24). The lingering effects of the COVID-19 pandemic were receding from daily consciousness, though variants remained a concern. In Denver specifically, there was moderate anticipation for the summer season and the approaching primaries; culturally, the Denver Nuggets basketball season had recently ended (they were eliminated from playoffs in early May), shifting local sports attention toward baseball and summer activities.
